name: Faust (Python Stream Processing)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD 3-Clause); Robinhood; actively maintained fork (faust-streaming)

auth.notes: Kafka auth via broker config; no additional auth for the library

performance.notes: Python asyncio-based; lower throughput than Java Kafka Streams but sufficient for most use cases; ~100k msg/sec

agent_readiness.notes: Python stream processing framework inspired by Kafka Streams. Agents (async generators) process Kafka topics. Windowing, aggregation, table state stores (backed by RocksDB). Channels for in-process streams. Built on asyncio. Used by Robinhood for financial data pipelines. @app.agent(topic) decorator model. Tables for stateful processing with changelog topics. Can be used with any Kafka-compatible broker (Confluent, Redpanda). faust-streaming is the actively maintained fork.

gotchas: Original Robinhood Faust is abandoned — use faust-streaming fork
RocksDB dependency for stateful tables (requires librocksdb)
Lower throughput than Java Kafka Streams for high-volume use cases
asyncio-based — requires async programming model throughout
best_when: Python-native Kafka stream processing without Java
Stateful stream processing with windowing and aggregation
Teams wanting Kafka Streams semantics in Python async code
avoid_when: Very high throughput (use Java Kafka Streams or Flink)
Non-Kafka event streams (use bytewax for Kinesis/HTTP sources)
summary: Faust (faust-streaming fork) is Python's Kafka Streams equivalent — async stream processing with @app.agent decorators, windowing, aggregation, and stateful table stores (RocksDB). Built on asyncio for non-blocking I/O. Used by Robinhood for financial data pipelines. Lower throughput than Java Kafka Streams but excellent for Python teams needing Kafka stream processing without JVM complexity.
